knowledge_base_articles.title: Troubleshooting Overheating on ProRack Server X200
knowledge_base_articles.content:
This article helps you diagnose and resolve overheating issues on the ProRack Server X200.

**Symptoms**
- Server fans running at maximum speed for extended periods
- CPU or system temperature warnings in the BMC/iDRAC logs
- Unexpected thermal shutdowns under moderate load

**Root Causes**
Common reasons for overheating on the ProRack Server X200 include:
- Insufficient front-to-back airflow in the rack
- Blocked or missing drive bay fillers
- Accumulation of dust in heatsinks and air filters
- Ambient intake temperature above 27°C (80°F)

**Initial Checks**
1. Verify the server is mounted in a rack with at least 10 cm (4 in) clearance at the rear.
2. Confirm that no cables or objects obstruct the front or rear vents.
3. Check the BMC/iDRAC hardware logs for temperature and fan alerts.
4. Ensure all fan modules are present and reporting as healthy.

**Cooling and Airflow Recommendations**
- Maintain cold-aisle temperatures between 18–27°C.
- Use blanking panels in all unused rack spaces to prevent recirculation.
- Group ProRack Server X200 units in the same rack to simplify airflow design.

**Cleaning Procedure**
1. Power down the server during a maintenance window.
2. Remove the top cover and access air filters and heatsinks.
3. Use compressed air to clear dust from filters, fans, and heatsinks.
4. Reassemble the chassis and verify all panels are securely closed.

**Firmware and Fan Profile**
- Update the system BIOS and BMC firmware to the latest supported versions.
- Set the fan profile to "performance" if the server consistently operates at high load.

## Recommended Next Steps
For persistent overheating after following the checks above, review the **Recommended Next Steps** section below.

1. Capture and export a SupportAssist or hardware log bundle from the BMC.
2. Open a support case and attach recent thermal graphs and event logs.
3. Consider redistributing high-heat components (GPUs, NVMe drives) across multiple chassis.
4. Plan a rack-level airflow assessment if more than 20% of servers report thermal warnings.
knowledge_base_articles.excerpt: Learn how to diagnose and fix overheating issues on the ProRack Server X200, including airflow, cleaning, and firmware recommendations.
knowledge_base_articles.recommendedNextSteps: If your ProRack Server X200 continues to overheat after basic cleaning and airflow checks, export the latest BMC or iDRAC hardware logs, create a support case with those logs and recent thermal graphs, and schedule a rack-level airflow assessment. In environments with sustained high load, evaluate moving high-heat components to a different chassis or reducing the ambient intake temperature by 2–3°C.

knowledge_base_articles.title: ProRack Server X200 Rack Installation Best Practices
knowledge_base_articles.content:
This guide walks through the recommended steps for installing a ProRack Server X200 into a standard 19-inch rack.

Topics:
- Unboxing and inspection
- Rail kit installation
- Cable management and labeling
- Power redundancy and PDU selection
- Network connectivity and bonding options.
knowledge_base_articles.excerpt: Follow these best practices when mounting a ProRack Server X200 in your datacenter rack.
knowledge_base_articles.recommendedNextSteps: Verify that the rack rails are properly locked, the server weight is evenly distributed, and that both power supplies are connected to independent PDUs before powering on.

knowledge_base_articles.title: Updating Firmware on Rack Server R500
knowledge_base_articles.content:
This article describes how to update BIOS, BMC, and storage controller firmware on the Rack Server R500.

You can update firmware using:
- Bootable ISO image
- Operating system–based update utility
- Out-of-band BMC interface.

Always back up critical data and schedule a maintenance window before applying firmware updates.
knowledge_base_articles.excerpt: Step-by-step instructions to safely update firmware on Rack Server R500 systems.
knowledge_base_articles.recommendedNextSteps: After the update completes, verify firmware versions in the BMC, run a full reboot test, and monitor hardware logs for at least 24 hours.

knowledge_base_articles.title: Maximizing Battery Life on BizBook 14 Pro
knowledge_base_articles.content:
BizBook 14 Pro laptops include several power profiles designed for business users.

To maximize battery life:
- Enable "Eco" mode in the Power Center app.
- Lower display brightness to 40–60%.
- Disable unused radios such as Bluetooth when not required.
- Use the integrated GPU instead of the discrete GPU when on battery.
knowledge_base_articles.excerpt: Tips to extend battery life on BizBook 14 Pro business laptops.
knowledge_base_articles.recommendedNextSteps: Run the Battery Health Check utility monthly and recalibrate the battery if capacity drops below 80%.

knowledge_base_articles.title: Configuring VPN Access on BizBook 14 Pro
knowledge_base_articles.content:
This article explains how to configure Always-On VPN on BizBook 14 Pro devices for secure remote access.

It covers profile import, certificate installation, and split-tunnel vs full-tunnel configurations.
knowledge_base_articles.excerpt: Set up secure VPN connectivity on BizBook 14 Pro laptops.
knowledge_base_articles.recommendedNextSteps: Test the VPN profile on a non-production device before broad deployment and document the helpdesk process for password resets.

knowledge_base_articles.title: Improving Performance on EdgeBook 15 Business Laptops
knowledge_base_articles.content:
EdgeBook 15 Business systems ship with performance presets that can be tuned for your workload.

To improve performance:
- Switch the power profile to "Performance" when plugged in.
- Ensure that both RAM channels are populated for dual-channel operation.
- Update graphics and chipset drivers using the Device Manager utility.
knowledge_base_articles.excerpt: Optimize EdgeBook 15 Business systems for heavy office and development workloads.
knowledge_base_articles.recommendedNextSteps: Benchmark your typical workload before and after tuning, and document a standard configuration image for future deployments.

knowledge_base_articles.title: Creating Macros on the UltraKey MK700 Mechanical Keyboard
knowledge_base_articles.content:
The UltraKey MK700 Mechanical Keyboard supports per-key macros via the UltraKey Configurator software.

This article explains how to:
- Install UltraKey Configurator
- Record time-based macros
- Assign macros to function layers
- Export and import macro profiles.
knowledge_base_articles.excerpt: Use UltraKey Configurator to create powerful macros on the MK700 keyboard.
knowledge_base_articles.recommendedNextSteps: Back up your macro profiles regularly, especially before firmware updates or OS reinstalls.

knowledge_base_articles.title: Best Practices for Backing Up Production Servers
knowledge_base_articles.content:
This vendor-neutral article covers general best practices for backing up production servers.

Topics include:
- 3-2-1 backup rule
- On-site vs off-site backups
- Testing restores
- Retention policies and compliance.
knowledge_base_articles.excerpt: Follow these guidelines to protect critical server data with reliable backups.
knowledge_base_articles.recommendedNextSteps: Document your RPO/RTO targets, configure automated backup verification, and schedule periodic restore tests.

knowledge_base_articles.title: Diagnosing Network Latency in Hybrid Environments
knowledge_base_articles.content: Learn how to diagnose latency between on-premises servers and cloud workloads using built-in OS tools and third-party monitoring platforms.
knowledge_base_articles.excerpt: Step-by-step process to trace and resolve high network latency.
knowledge_base_articles.recommendedNextSteps: Capture packet traces during peak usage, correlate with application logs, and adjust QoS policies as needed.

knowledge_base_articles.title: Designing Proper Rack Airflow for High-Density Servers
knowledge_base_articles.content:
Proper airflow design is critical for high-density racks containing ProRack Server X200 and Rack Server R500 units.

This article explains hot-aisle/cold-aisle concepts, blanking panel placement, and cable management strategies to avoid recirculation.
knowledge_base_articles.excerpt: Ensure reliable cooling for high-density server racks with proper airflow planning.
knowledge_base_articles.recommendedNextSteps: Perform a thermal imaging survey of critical racks and correct any hotspots or bypass airflow.

knowledge_base_articles.title: Handling Disk Predictive Failures on ProRack Server X200
knowledge_base_articles.content:
When the ProRack Server X200 reports a predictive disk failure, you should replace the affected drive as soon as possible.

This guide covers identifying the failed drive, capturing logs, and performing an online replacement in RAID configurations.
knowledge_base_articles.excerpt: Identify and replace failing disks on ProRack Server X200 arrays without downtime.
knowledge_base_articles.recommendedNextSteps: Schedule a drive replacement at the next maintenance window and confirm the RAID rebuild completes successfully.

knowledge_base_articles.title: Troubleshooting USB-C Docking Issues
knowledge_base_articles.content: This article covers common USB-C docking problems such as displays not waking, intermittent disconnects, and power delivery failures. It applies to BizBook and EdgeBook laptop families using third-party USB-C docks.
knowledge_base_articles.excerpt: Resolve common USB-C docking issues with BizBook and EdgeBook laptops.
knowledge_base_articles.recommendedNextSteps: Update dock firmware, replace any damaged cables, and ensure the dock is connected to the primary USB-C port recommended in the laptop manual.
